tool.name: seqcount
tool.summary: Count sequence records in one local sequence input

sections.title: Overview
sections.content: `seqcount` counts sequence records through the shared Epithema sequence IO layer instead of embedding format-specific counting logic in the tool. The same code path supports the current primary sequence readers for FASTA, FASTQ, EMBL, and GenBank inputs.

sections.title: Inputs
sections.content: The current v1 interface accepts one local sequence input path. Input format is inferred from extension when possible and otherwise from leading file content through the shared sequence-stream detection path.

sections.title: Outputs
sections.content: The tool emits one stable two-column table report with `input` and `count`. CLI output also includes the standard Epithema result summary lines describing the input path, record count, and tabular output format.

sections.title: Current Status
sections.content: This method is implemented and exposed through `epithema seqcount`. Validation currently covers single-record and multi-record FASTA inputs plus malformed and empty-input failure behavior.

sections.title: Caveats
sections.content: The first release supports one input path per invocation. Multiple-input aggregation and per-input grouped reporting remain deferred. Empty or malformed inputs fail clearly instead of returning an implicit zero count.

artifacts.label: Three-record FASTA fixture
artifacts.description: Repository-managed multi-record FASTA fixture used to validate deterministic counting.

examples.title: Count records in a three-record FASTA input
examples.description: Counts a small multi-record FASTA fixture and returns one stable table row with the source path and record count.

expected_outputs.label: Sequence count report
expected_outputs.description: A single-row table report containing the input path and the deterministic record count.
